utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello larry how are you doing today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been better my primary care doctor wanted me to see you for this back pain that i've been having for a couple of years now

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and i have tried so many things and just nothing seems to work

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm sorry to hear that now do you remember what caused the pain initially

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you know i never fell or was in an accident but i do remember it all happened like i started having back pain after i was moving furniture one weekend

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and now can you describe the pain for me

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah it feels like a sharp stabbing pain in my back and it does go down even down to my right leg sometimes all the way down to my toe it feels like my big toe

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and i also feel like i just ca n't get comfortable which is worse when i sit down okay yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay now with that do you have any numbness or tingling associated with your back pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i do get some numbness and tingling especially to my right leg that goes down to my foot

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: let me separate these

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how about a loss of sensation in the genital or the rectal area

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no nothing like that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay now do you have any weakness

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i would n't say i have any weakness no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay do you experience like any loss of control of your bladder or your bowels

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay now you mentioned earlier that you you have some tried tried some things in the past so tell me what were they

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i've tried some physical therapy before and so when it gets really bad they've even had to prescribe some strong pain medications for me but that was only temporary and they even mentioned surgery to me in the past but i really would like to avoid surgery

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay sure so tell me what is your day like

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my day so i try to be as active as i can but of course it's been difficult with my back pain and so because of that i have gained some weight over the past years

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright well i'm sorry to hear that i know that you used to like playing golf

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i and you know golf is relatively new for me i've been trying to get into it but this has definitely set me back

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i'm sorry yep probably now at the peak of you know just learning it and being able to do more with it this comes and this happens right

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: exactly

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well let's see what we can do for you here so you can go out into the you know the golf course again now do you tell me do you have any family members with spine conditions

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i ca n't recall any family members

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and do you smoke

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not now i i quit about twenty years ago

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright well alright well i like to do a quick physical exam on you here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na go ahead here i reviewed your vitals and they overall look pretty good so that's a good sign now on your musculoskeletal back exam there is no overlying erythema and that just means redness or ecchymosis and that just means bruising of the skin now there is midline tenderness at l4 l5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness so do me a favor and i'm gon na just have you you know bend forward and straighten up a bit

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay that's painful

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sorry so definitely some pain with lumbar flexion and supine straight leg test is positive so on your neurological examination there is decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at l4 dermatome okay and i'm just gon na take a look at your knees here real quick and then your your ankles so patellar and achilles reflexes are symmetrical so good now i reviewed the results of your mri and it shows disk herniation with nerve impingement so let me tell you about my assessment and plan here larry so for your problem of chronic lumbar back pain so based on your symptoms your exam and reviewing your mri results your symptoms are consistent with an l4 l5 herniated disk with radicul radiculopathy so this means that one of your disks that sits between your spinal vertebrae is bulging out and this bulge is pinching on some nerves and that's why you feel that tingling in your leg so yeah yeah so i am recommending a spinal injection with a strong inflammatory medication called a corticosteroid and what i do is i will inject the corticosteroid into that lumbar spine under fluoroscopy which you can think of you know an x-ray to help localize to the exact area of your pain now this should this should help the inflammation that is causing the pain so what do you think about that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: is there something i'm gon na have to do all the time

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well you know patients are different so what we do is we will monitor you and we see how you respond to the injection and the patient's intervals can be different as well but we usually do not do more than four times a year

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright and it's not a long procedure it's it's relatively quick and common and you should be out of the office within an hour

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alright sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright well let's go ahead and get you started on the first one today if that works for you if not we'll get you scheduled to start your first injection

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alright thank you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright thank you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: bye

detail_list.summary: Larry Garcia is a 41-year-old male presenting for evaluation of back pain.
Back pain
The doctor asks about the initial cause of the back pain.
The patient reports back pain began 2 years ago after moving furniture, with no specific injury.
The doctor asks the patient to describe the pain.
The patient describes sharp, stabbing pain in the lower back radiating to the right leg and great toe.
The patient reports difficulty getting comfortable, which worsens with sitting.
The doctor asks about numbness or tingling.
The patient reports numbness and tingling in the right leg radiating to the foot.
The doctor asks about loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area.
The patient denies loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area.
The doctor asks about weakness.
The patient denies weakness.
The doctor asks about loss of bladder or bowel control.
The patient denies loss of bladder or bowel control.
The patient has tried physical therapy and past pain medications, and wishes to avoid surgery.
The patient reports difficulty staying active and weight gain over the past year.
The doctor asks about family history of spine conditions.
The patient denies family history of spine conditions.
The doctor asks about smoking status.
The patient quit smoking 20 years ago.
The doctor notes normal vitals, no erythema or ecchymosis, and midline tenderness at L4-5 with right-sided paravertebral tenderness.
The patient has pain with lumbar flexion, positive straight leg test, decreased sensation at L4 dermatome, and symmetrical reflexes.
MRI shows disc herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment is L4-5 herniated disc with radiculopathy.
The doctor recommends a corticosteroid injection under fluoroscopy.
The doctor asks if the patient agrees to the injection plan.
The patient asks if the injection is a recurring procedure.
The doctor explains the injection is performed as needed, typically no more than four times a year.
The doctor offers to perform the first injection today or schedule it for later.
